repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Summary

Advances the Phase 2 work tracked in #38445 for UI2 messages/pager behavior, focused on:

- introducing/refining `'messagesheight'` behavior for displayed message area height
- aligning pager focus/interaction behavior with the issue discussion around `'more'` semantics
- updating related UI/message handling paths and tests accordingly

## Related issue

- Addresses #38445

## What changed

- Added/updated logic for message display height handling (`'messagesheight'`).
- Adjusted pager/message interaction behavior in line with UI2 direction discussed in #38445.
- Updated tests/documentation where applicable.

## Validation

## Manual Verification

### 1. Message height limit

```vim
:set messagesheight=2
:echo "1\n2\n3" 
```

### Expected: 
Only first 2 lines are displayed

### 2. Non-blocking behavior
 ```vim 
:set nomore
:echo join(range(10), "\n")
```
### Expected: 
- Output appears as overlay
- Cursor remains in current buffer

### 3. Page focus (opt-in)
 ```vim
:set more
:echo join(range(20), "\n")
```
### Expected 
Pager takes focus

### 4. Explicit pager

```vim
:g<
```

### Expected
Full pager opens


### Test results

| Target | Result |
|---|---|
| `make unittest` | 774 passed, 6 skipped, 0 failed |
| `make functionaltest` |8407 passed, 68 skipped, 4 failed |
| `make lintlua` | passed (after auto-fixing 2 `stylua` diffs) |
| `make oldtest` | N/A (no legacy `test/old/testdir` changes) |



### Functional test failures (non-regression)

The 4 functional failures are pre-existing/environment-sensitive and not introduced by this PR:

- `lsp_spec` hostname-related test
- `optwin_spec` options snapshot
- `tui_spec` screen comparison (2 tests)

## Notes

- No legacy Vimscript testdir changes in this PR.
- If maintainers want, I can split follow-up cleanup/refactor parts into separate PRs.

AI-assisted: GitHub Copilot

comment_data.body: `messagesheight` introduces new user-facing behavior (message area truncation / overlay vs pager) but this spec file does not currently exercise `:set messagesheight=...` at all. Please add at least one functional test that sets `messagesheight` to a small value and asserts the visible message area is limited and overflow behavior matches the intended UI2 semantics (including interaction with 'more').
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -321,17 +324,18 @@ function M.show_msg(tgt, kind, content, replace_last, append, id)
   if tgt == 'msg' then
     api.nvim_win_set_width(ui.wins.msg, width)
     local texth = api.nvim_win_text_height(ui.wins.msg, { start_row = start_row, end_row = row })
-    if texth.all > math.ceil(o.lines * 0.5) then
-      M.expand_msg(tgt)
+    if texth.all > o.messagesheight then
+      expand_msg(tgt)
     else

comment_data.body:
The description says "The pager is never focused by default" but also states that setting 'more' will focus the pager on overflow. Since 'more' defaults to on in Nvim, this wording is confusing/arguably incorrect. Consider rephrasing to explicitly describe both modes (e.g. "When 'more' is off, overflow shows an unfocused overlay…; when 'more' is on, overflow focuses the pager").
```suggestion
        current buffer.

        When 'more' is off, the pager is not focused; to see overflow content,
        use |g<|.

        When 'more' is on (the default), and messages exceed 'messagesheight',
        the pager is focused automatically.

        The user cursor position remains in whatever buffer was active when
        the message-triggering mapping or command was invoked.

        The message area disappears on any kind of user input. It is not
        timer-based.
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5815,6 +5815,30 @@ local options = {
       type = 'string',
       varname = 'p_mopt',
     },
+    {
+      abbreviation = 'msh',
+      defaults = 5,
+      desc = [=[
+        Maximum number of lines for messages displayed in the message area.
+        Messages are shown up to 'messagesheight' lines and may overlay the
+        current buffer. The pager is never focused by default; to see overflow
+        content, use |g<|.
+
+        The user cursor position remains in whatever buffer was active when
+        the message-triggering mapping or command was invoked.
+
+        The message area disappears on any kind of user input. It is not
+        timer-based.
+
+        Set 'more' to automatically focus the pager when messages exceed
+        'messagesheight'.

comment_data.body: The help text states "The pager is never focused by default" while also describing 'more' as the switch that focuses the pager on overflow. Because 'more' defaults to on, this reads as contradictory for default settings. Please reword this section to describe the behavior in terms of 'more' being on vs off, rather than claiming the pager is "never focused by default".
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4390,6 +4390,23 @@ A jump table for the options with a short description can be found at |Q_op|.
 	generated from a list of items, e.g., the Buffers menu.  Changing this
 	option has no direct effect, the menu must be refreshed first.
 
+					*'messagesheight'* *'msh'*
+'messagesheight' 'msh'	number	(default 5)
+			global
+	Maximum number of lines for messages displayed in the message area.
+	Messages are shown up to 'messagesheight' lines and may overlay the
+	current buffer. The pager is never focused by default; to see overflow
+	content, use |g<|.

comment_data.body:
This option doc comment says "The pager is never focused by default" while the subsequent line indicates 'more' will auto-focus the pager on overflow. Since 'more' defaults to true, this is confusing. Consider rephrasing the Lua meta docs to describe the two behaviors explicitly based on whether 'more' is on/off.
```suggestion
--- current buffer.
---
--- When 'more' is off, the pager is not focused automatically; to see
--- overflow content, use |g<|.
--- When 'more' is on (the default), the pager is automatically focused
--- when messages exceed 'messagesheight'.
---
--- The user cursor position remains in whatever buffer was active when
--- the message-triggering mapping or command was invoked.
---
--- The message area disappears on any kind of user input. It is not
--- timer-based.
---
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4445,6 +4445,27 @@ vim.o.mis = vim.o.menuitems
 vim.go.menuitems = vim.o.menuitems
 vim.go.mis = vim.go.menuitems
 
+--- Maximum number of lines for messages displayed in the message area.
+--- Messages are shown up to 'messagesheight' lines and may overlay the
+--- current buffer. The pager is never focused by default; to see overflow
+--- content, use `g<`.
+---
+--- The user cursor position remains in whatever buffer was active when
+--- the message-triggering mapping or command was invoked.
+---
+--- The message area disappears on any kind of user input. It is not
+--- timer-based.
+---
+--- Set 'more' to automatically focus the pager when messages exceed
+--- 'messagesheight'.
+---

comment_data.body:
`vim.on_key()` returns a namespace id, and using `ui.ns` as the fallback here can overwrite the `cmd_on_key` handler (which also uses `ui.ns`) while the pager overlay is active. That can leave the overlay undisposable or break expanded-cmdline dismissal if asynchronous messages reconfigure on_key handlers. Prefer creating/retaining a dedicated namespace for the pager overlay (e.g. call `vim.on_key(pager_on_key_fn, M.pager_on_key)` and let it allocate an id when nil) instead of reusing `ui.ns`.
```suggestion
            M.pager_on_key = vim.on_key(pager_on_key_fn, M.pager_on_key)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -663,11 +680,26 @@ function M.set_pos(tgt)
       elseif tgt == 'msg' then
         -- Ensure last line is visible and first line is at top of window.
         fn.win_execute(ui.wins.msg, 'norm! Gzb')
-      elseif tgt == 'pager' and not in_pager then
-        enter_pager()
+      elseif tgt == 'pager' then
+        if not in_pager then
+          if focus or vim.o.more then
+            -- Clear any overlay state before focusing the pager.
+            if M.pager_on_key then
+              vim.on_key(nil, M.pager_on_key)
+              M.pager_on_key = nil
+            end
+
+            enter_pager()
+          else
+            -- 'more' is off: show pager as unfocused overlay, dismissed on next keypress.
+            M.pager_on_key = vim.on_key(pager_on_key_fn, M.pager_on_key or ui.ns)

comment_data.body: If the user sets `messagesheight` to 0 or a negative value, this clamp can produce a window height <= 0, which will cause `nvim_win_set_config()` to error (floating window height must be >= 1). Please harden this logic by enforcing a minimum height (e.g. treat values < 1 as 1, or hide the message windows when 0 is intended).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -579,6 +590,10 @@ local was_cmdwin = ''
 local function win_row_height(tgt, min)
   local cfgmin = ui.cfg.msg[tgt].height --[[@as number]]
   cfgmin = cfgmin > 1 and cfgmin or math.ceil(o.lines * cfgmin)
+  -- For msg and cmd (expanded) targets, respect the 'messagesheight' option.
+  if tgt == 'msg' or tgt == 'cmd' then
+    cfgmin = math.min(cfgmin, o.messagesheight)
+  end

comment_data.body:
The `cmd_on_key` handler opens the pager when `<CR>` is used to "enter" the expanded cmdline, but it no longer consumes that keypress. This can leak the `<CR>` into the underlying editor state (e.g. moving the cursor / triggering another action) after `norm! g<` runs. Consider returning an empty string when `entered` is true (similar to the previous behavior) so the key used to enter the pager is not processed twice.
```suggestion
    api.nvim_command('norm! g<')
    -- Consume the key used to enter the pager so it is not processed twice.
    return ''
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -527,7 +539,6 @@ local cmd_on_key = function(_, typed)
     api.nvim_command('norm! g<')

comment_regular_data.body:
The PR introduces messagesheight with a default of 5, but the existing tests in **[messages2_spec.lua]**  were written without any height limit on messages. Tests **41,** **691,** and **763** now produce different screen snapshots because the default messagesheight=5 constrains layout that those tests relied on being unconstrained.
The existing tests were written when there was no height limit on messages. Now that the default is 5 lines, the message area is capped, which changes screen layouts in unrelated tests.

This is premature (and seems to contain a bunch of unnecessary (AI generated?) changes). We will iterate on the `opts` table of the ui2 module in the next release cycle to see which options need to be promoted to the option subsystem if/when UI2 is made default. We don't need parallel ways to configure UI2.
